one:Page.name: Server - Migrating AD Accounts from RC4 to AES

one:OE.author: Mike Row
one:OE.one:T: Server - Migrating AD Accounts from RC4 to AES

Microsoft is deprecating RC4 encryption for Kerberos. Starting with the April 2026 cumulative update, domain controllers enter enforcement mode and stop accepting RC4 by default. The July 2026 update makes this permanent with no rollback.
Any account where msDS-SupportedEncryptionTypes is empty or 0 currently falls back to RC4. These accounts will be impacted.
The attribute is a bitmask. Add the bit values together to get the decimal value you set on the account.
Use 28 if you need a safety net while testing. Use 24 when you are confident everything supports AES.
Step 2: Set to AES-only (or 28 for transitional)
For computer accounts use Set-ADComputer instead.
This is critical. Without a password reset, AES keys will not be generated in AD regardless of the value you set.
Event ID 4769 - look for encryption type 0x17 (RC4). You want to see 0x12 (AES256).
Event ID 201/202 (System log) - RC4 usage warnings added in the January 2026 updates.
Decrypting the Selection of Supported Kerberos Encryption Types - bitmask values and Kerberos encryption explained
AD Hardening Part 4: Enforcing AES for Kerberos - migration sequencing guidance
Detect and Remediate RC4 Usage in Kerberos - official detection scripts
What Is Going On with RC4 in Kerberos? - April/July 2026 enforcement timeline
Beyond RC4 for Windows Authentication - Microsoft security baseline values and detection tooling
